sections.intro: The CSS 'Cheat Sheet' for fast recall. Focus on these to clear technical rounds with confidence.

cards.title: Browser Rendering: Layout vs Paint vs Composite
cards.contentHtml:
• Layout (Reflow): Calculating geometry. Triggered by width, height, top/left. Expensive.
  • Paint: Filling in pixels. Triggered by color, box-shadow.
  • Composite: Combining layers on the GPU. Triggered by transform and opacity.
  • Mastery Tip: Always animate with Transform and Opacity to avoid the main thread and ensure 60fps.

cards.title: CSS Logical Properties & Writing Modes
cards.contentHtml:
• Physical vs Logical: margin-left vs margin-inline-start.
  • Purpose: Automatically adjusts layout for Right-to-Left (RTL) languages or vertical writing modes without extra CSS.
  • Modern Standard: Use inline (horizontal) and block (vertical) instead of top/bottom/left/right.

cards.title: Advanced Selectors: :is(), :where(), :has()
cards.contentHtml:
• :is(): Reduces repetition (:is(h1, h2, h3):hover). Matches the specificity of its highest selector.
  • :where(): Same as :is() but with Zero Specificity. Perfect for CSS resets.
  • :has() (The Parent Selector): Enables styling an element based on its children (e.g., .card:has(img) { padding: 0; }).

cards.title: Container Queries (@container)
cards.contentHtml:
• Mental Model: Querying the Parent's Width instead of the Viewport Width.
  • Use Case: A component that looks different when placed in a narrow sidebar vs a wide main content area.
  • Units: cqw (container query width), cqh, cqmin, cqmax.

cards.title: CSS Variables (Custom Properties) Mastery
cards.contentHtml:
• Scope: Variables follow the cascade. Defining in :root makes them global; defining in a class makes them local/scoped.
  • Runtime Updates: Can be updated via JavaScript (element.style.setProperty()) for dynamic theming.
  • Fallback: Support defaults like var(--main-color, #000).

cards.title: Modern Color Architecture (OKLCH)
cards.contentHtml:
• OKLCH: A perceptually uniform color space. Lightness (L) feels the same across all hues.
  • P3 Gamut: Modern monitors (Macs/iPhones) support 50% more colors than standard sRGB.
  • Relative Colors: (v2024+) color: rgb(from var(--primary) r g b / 0.5)—modify colors on the fly.

cards.title: Grid vs Flexbox: The Layout Algorithm
cards.contentHtml:
• Flexbox (1D): Content-driven. Items define their own size; the container distributes. Best for menus/toolbars.
  • Grid (2D): Container-driven. The container defines the tracks; items are placed in them. Best for page layouts.
  • Subgrid: Allows child items to align perfectly with the parent's grid tracks.

cards.title: Performance: Containment & Will-Change
cards.contentHtml:
• Contain: Tells the browser that an element's subtree is isolated from the rest of the page. High performance win for complex DOMs.
  • Will-change: Tells the browser to promote a layer to the GPU before the animation starts. Only use for frequently changing elements to avoid memory bloat.

cards.title: Modern 2025 Features: Anchor Positioning
cards.contentHtml:
• Anchor Positioning: Tethering one element to another without JavaScript (e.g., a tooltip to its button).
  • Scroll-Driven Animations: Native CSS animations that trigger based on scroll progress (scroll-timeline) instead of time.
  • Light-Dismiss: Combining native POP-OVER with CSS for zero-JS modals.

cards.title: Architecture & Methodology Trade-offs
cards.contentHtml:
• BEM: High predictability, verbose classes, prevents specificity wars.
  • Utility-First (Tailwind): Fast development, tiny CSS bundles, move styling into the HTML.
  • CSS-in-JS: Dynamic styling based on props, but comes with runtime performance overhead and bundle bloat.

sections.intro: CSS controls how everything looks. Master the box model, layout, and modern features.

cards.title: The Box Model — Foundation of Everything
cards.contentHtml:
/* Every element is a box with 4 layers: */
/* content → padding → border → margin */

/* DEFAULT (content-box): width = content only */
.box {{ width: 200px; padding: 20px; border: 2px solid; }}
/* Actual width = 200 + 20*2 + 2*2 = 244px 😫 */

/* BORDER-BOX: width includes padding + border */
*, *::before, *::after {{ box-sizing: border-box; }}
.box {{ width: 200px; padding: 20px; border: 2px solid; }}
/* Actual width = 200px ✅ (content shrinks to fit) */

/* Margin collapsing — margins DON'T add, they overlap */
.a {{ margin-bottom: 20px; }}
.b {{ margin-top: 30px; }}
/* Gap between a and b = 30px (not 50px!) */
/* Collapsing happens only vertically, not in flex/grid */
Always set box-sizing: border-box globally. It's the first line in every modern CSS reset. Without it, padding/border calculations will constantly surprise you.

cards.title: Flexbox — Complete Reference
cards.contentHtml:
/* Container properties */
.flex {{ 
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: row | column | row-reverse | column-reverse;
  justify-content: flex-start | center | flex-end | space-between | space-around | space-evenly;
  align-items: stretch | flex-start | center | flex-end | baseline;
  flex-wrap: nowrap | wrap | wrap-reverse;
  gap: 1rem;              /* gap between items */
  align-content: stretch | center | space-between; /* multi-line */
}}

/* Item properties */
.item {{
  flex: 1;              /* shorthand: flex-grow flex-shrink flex-basis */
  flex-grow: 1;          /* how much to grow (0 = don't) */
  flex-shrink: 0;         /* how much to shrink (0 = don't) */
  flex-basis: 200px;      /* starting size before grow/shrink */
  align-self: center;     /* override align-items for this item */
  order: -1;             /* visual reorder (don't use for a11y) */
}}

/* Common patterns */
/* Center anything */
.center {{ display: flex; justify-content: center; align-items: center; }}

/* Sticky footer */
body {{ display: flex; flex-direction: column; min-height: 100vh; }}
main {{ flex: 1; }}

/* Space between with last item pushed right */
.nav {{ display: flex; gap: 1rem; }}
.nav .logout {{ margin-left: auto; }}
justify-content = alignment along the MAIN axis (horizontal in row, vertical in column). align-items = alignment along the CROSS axis (vertical in row, horizontal in column). Think: justify = main, align = cross.

cards.title: CSS Grid — Complete Reference
cards.contentHtml:
/* Container */
.grid {{
  display: grid;
  grid-template-columns: 1fr 2fr 1fr;          /* 3 columns */
  grid-template-columns: repeat(3, 1fr);       /* same thing */
  grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fill, minmax(250px, 1fr)); /* responsive! */
  grid-template-rows: auto 1fr auto;
  gap: 1rem;
  
  /* Named areas */
  grid-template-areas:
    "header header header"
    "sidebar main   aside"
    "footer footer  footer";
}}

/* Items */
.header {{ grid-area: header; }}
.sidebar {{ grid-area: sidebar; }}
.main {{ grid-area: main; }}

/* Spanning */
.hero {{ grid-column: 1 / -1; }}     /* full width */
.wide {{ grid-column: span 2; }}      /* span 2 columns */

/* Auto-placement */
.masonry {{ 
  grid-template-columns: repeat(3, 1fr);
  grid-auto-rows: minmax(100px, auto);
}}

/* Subgrid (child inherits parent's grid lines) */
.child {{ 
  display: grid;
  grid-template-columns: subgrid; /* align with parent's columns */
}}
repeat(auto-fill, minmax(250px, 1fr)) is the holy grail of responsive layouts — creates as many 250px+ columns as fit, no media queries needed. auto-fit is similar but collapses empty tracks.

cards.title: Positioning
cards.contentHtml:
/* static — default, normal flow */
/* relative — offset from normal position, creates stacking context */
/* absolute — removed from flow, positioned to nearest positioned ancestor */
/* fixed — positioned to viewport (stays on scroll) */
/* sticky — hybrid: normal until scroll threshold, then fixed */

.sticky-nav {{
  position: sticky;
  top: 0;              /* sticks when scrolled to top */
  z-index: 100;
}}

.modal-overlay {{
  position: fixed;
  inset: 0;            /* shorthand for top/right/bottom/left: 0 */
  z-index: 1000;
}}

.tooltip {{
  position: absolute;
  top: 100%;           /* below the parent */
  left: 50%;
  transform: translateX(-50%); /* center horizontally */
}}
BWhat is the difference between strong/em and b/i?+

<strong>: semantic importance (screen readers emphasize). <b>: bold, no meaning. <em>: emphasis (screen readers change tone). <i>: italic, no meaning. Also: <small> = fine print, <s> = no longer accurate, <del>/<ins> = tracked changes, <mark> = highlighted.

BWhat are global HTML attributes?+

Valid on ANY element: id, class, style, title (tooltip), tabindex, hidden, draggable, contenteditable, dir, lang, data-*, role, aria-*, slot, part, translate, spellcheck, autofocus, enterkeyhint, inputmode, popover, inert, nonce. Know these — they're asked frequently.

BWhat heading levels should you use and why?+

One <h1> per page (main title, critical for SEO). <h2-h6> in order — never skip levels. Screen readers let users jump between headings. Search engines use the hierarchy. Use CSS to control visual size — don't pick heading level based on appearance.

BWhat is the difference between meter and progress?+

<progress value=70 max=100>: task completion (upload, loading). <meter value=0.7 min=0 max=1 low=0.3 high=0.8>: measurement within a range (disk usage, score). Progress = something happening over time. Meter = static gauge.

BWhat are the anchor tag's key attributes?+

href (URL, #hash, mailto:, tel:), target (_self, _blank), rel (noopener, noreferrer, nofollow), download (triggers download), hreflang (linked page language). For _blank, always add rel="noopener" — without it, the new page can access window.opener (security risk).

IWhat is the Popover API?+

<button popovertarget="id"> + <div popover>. Native show/hide with: light-dismiss (click outside closes), Escape key, focus management, top-layer rendering. auto = light-dismiss, manual = explicit only. Replaces many dropdown/tooltip JS libraries. Supported in all modern browsers since 2024.

IWhat is the hidden="until-found" attribute?+

Content is hidden but findable by browser find-in-page (Ctrl+F) and anchor links. When found, it automatically reveals. Perfect for collapsed/accordion content that should remain searchable. Regular hidden makes content completely invisible to find-in-page.

IWhat is the sandbox attribute on iframes?+

Restricts iframe capabilities: sandbox="allow-scripts allow-same-origin". Without values, ALL restrictions apply (no scripts, forms, popups, navigation, plugins). Add permissions back as needed. Essential for securely embedding untrusted third-party content.

IWhat is fieldset/legend and when do you use them?+

<fieldset> + <legend> groups related form controls with a semantic label. Screen readers announce: "Contact Information group: Name field, Email field." A plain div has no grouping semantics. Use for: radio button groups, checkbox groups, address sections, any cluster of related fields.

AWhat are all input types and their mobile behaviors?+

email: @ key. tel: phone dialer. url: / and .com. number: number pad (NOT for phone — use tel). search: search icon. date/time/datetime-local: native pickers. color: color picker. range: slider. file: file browser. Tip: inputmode="numeric" on text input gives number keyboard WITHOUT the spinner arrows of type=number.

AWhat is the Sanitizer API?+

Browser-native HTML sanitization: removes dangerous elements (script, iframe) and attributes (onclick, onerror) while preserving safe content. Replaces DOMPurify. Configurable allow/deny lists. Not yet widely supported but the future of XSS prevention for user-generated content displayed via innerHTML.

sections.intro: Advanced selectors, combinators, and pseudo-elements — the tools of CSS mastery.

cards.title: All CSS Selectors — Complete Reference
cards.contentHtml:
/* SIMPLE */
*                  /* Universal — all elements */
div                /* Type — all divs */
.class             /* Class */
#id                /* ID */
[attr]             /* Has attribute */
[attr="val"]       /* Exact match */
[attr^="val"]      /* Starts with */
[attr$="val"]      /* Ends with */
[attr*="val"]      /* Contains */
[attr~="val"]      /* Word in space-separated list */
[attr|="val"]      /* Starts with val or val- */
[attr="val" i]     /* Case insensitive */

/* COMBINATORS */
div p              /* Descendant (any depth) */
div > p            /* Direct child only */
h2 + p             /* Adjacent sibling (immediately after) */
h2 ~ p             /* General sibling (any after) */

/* PSEUDO-CLASSES — state based */
:hover :focus :active :visited
:focus-visible       /* Keyboard focus only (not mouse) */
:focus-within        /* Parent has focused child */
:first-child :last-child :only-child
:nth-child(2n)      /* Even children */
:nth-child(3n+1)    /* Every 3rd starting from 1st */
:nth-of-type(2)     /* 2nd of its type */
:not(.class)         /* Negation */
:empty               /* No children/text */
:checked :disabled :required :valid :invalid
:target              /* Element matching URL hash */
:is(h1, h2, h3)     /* Matches any (specificity = highest) */
:where(h1, h2, h3)  /* Same but ZERO specificity */
:has(.child)         /* Parent selector! */

/* PSEUDO-ELEMENTS — generated content */
::before ::after       /* Require content property */
::first-line ::first-letter
::placeholder          /* Input placeholder text */
::selection            /* User-selected text */
::marker               /* List bullet/number */
::backdrop             /* Dialog/fullscreen overlay */
::file-selector-button /* File input button */

cards.title: The :has() Selector — Deep Dive
cards.contentHtml:
/* Style parent based on children */
.card:has(img) {{ display: grid; grid-template-rows: auto 1fr; }}
.card:has(.badge) {{ border-color: gold; }}

/* Form validation without JS */
form:has(:invalid) .submit-btn {{ opacity: 0.5; pointer-events: none; }}
.field:has(:invalid:not(:placeholder-shown)) .error {{ display: block; }}

/* Sibling relationships */
h2:has(+ p) {{ margin-bottom: 0.5rem; }}  /* h2 followed by p */
li:has(+ li) {{ border-bottom: 1px solid; }}  /* all li except last */

/* Complex conditions */
body:has(dialog[open]) {{ overflow: hidden; }}  /* prevent scroll when modal open */
.grid:has(> :nth-child(4)) {{ grid-template-columns: repeat(2, 1fr); }}
/* 2-column grid ONLY when 4+ children exist */
:has() replaces SO much JavaScript. Form validation styling, conditional layouts, parent styling based on state — all pure CSS now. It's the most impactful CSS selector since flexbox.

cards.title: Pseudo-Elements — Advanced Patterns
cards.contentHtml:
/* Decorative line */
h2::after {{
  content: '';
  display: block;
  width: 60px; height: 3px;
  background: var(--color-primary);
  margin-top: 0.5rem;
}}

/* Required field indicator */
label:has(+ input:required)::after {{
  content: '*';
  color: var(--color-error);
  margin-inline-start: 0.25rem;
}}

/* Custom checkbox */
input[type="checkbox"] {{ appearance: none; width: 20px; height: 20px; border: 2px solid; border-radius: 4px; }}
input[type="checkbox"]:checked {{ background: var(--primary); }}
input[type="checkbox"]:checked::after {{
  content: '✓';
  display: grid; place-content: center;
  color: white; font-size: 14px;
}}

/* Tooltip with ::after */
[data-tooltip]:hover::after {{
  content: attr(data-tooltip);
  position: absolute; bottom: 100%;
  background: #333; color: white;
  padding: 4px 8px; border-radius: 4px;
  white-space: nowrap; font-size: 0.8rem;
}}

sections.intro: Modern CSS features that have changed how we write styles in 2025-2026.

cards.title: CSS Custom Properties (Variables)
cards.contentHtml:
/* Define on :root for global access */
:root {{
  --color-primary: #6366f1;
  --color-text: #1f2937;
  --spacing: 1rem;
  --radius: 8px;
  --shadow: 0 4px 12px rgb(0 0 0 / 0.1);
}}

/* Dark mode with variables */
@media (prefers-color-scheme: dark) {{
  :root {{
    --color-primary: #818cf8;
    --color-text: #e5e7eb;
  }}
}}

/* Component-scoped overrides */
.card {{ --card-padding: 1.5rem; padding: var(--card-padding); }}
.card.compact {{ --card-padding: 0.75rem; }}

/* With fallbacks */
color: var(--undefined-var, #000);

cards.title: Container Queries
cards.contentHtml:
/* Parent defines a container */
.card-grid {{
  container-type: inline-size;
  container-name: card-grid;
}}

/* Children respond to CONTAINER width, not viewport */
@container card-grid (min-width: 600px) {{
  .card {{ flex-direction: row; }}
}}

@container (min-width: 400px) {{
  .card-title {{ font-size: 1.5rem; }}
}}

/* Container query units */
.text {{ font-size: 3cqi; }} /* 3% of container inline size */
Media queries ask 'how wide is the screen?' Container queries ask 'how wide is my parent?' This is revolutionary for reusable components — a card can adapt whether it's in a sidebar or main content area.

cards.title: Modern Selectors & At-Rules
cards.contentHtml:
/* :has() — the parent selector (game changer!) */
.card:has(img) {{ grid-template-rows: auto 1fr; }}  /* only if card has image */
.form:has(:invalid) .submit {{ opacity: 0.5; }}  /* dim submit if form invalid */
h2:has(+ p) {{ margin-bottom: 0.5rem; }}           /* h2 followed by p */

/* :is() and :where() — group selectors */
:is(h1, h2, h3) {{ font-family: serif; }}
/* :where() is same but zero specificity */

/* Nesting (native CSS, no Sass needed!) */
.card {{
  padding: 1rem;
  & .title {{ font-size: 1.5rem; }}
  &:hover {{ background: #f5f5f5; }}
  & > .body {{ flex: 1; }}
  @media (width >= 768px) {{ padding: 2rem; }}
}}

/* @layer — control cascade order */
@layer base, components, utilities;
@layer base {{ a {{ color: blue; }} }}
@layer utilities {{ .text-red {{ color: red; }} }}

/* @scope — scoped styles */
@scope (.card) to (.card-footer) {{
  p {{ color: gray; }} /* only paragraphs between .card and .card-footer */
}}
:has() is the most powerful CSS selector ever added. It lets you style a parent based on its children — something that was impossible for 25 years. It enables things that previously required JavaScript.

cards.title: Animations & Transitions
cards.contentHtml:
/* Transitions — smooth state changes */
.btn {{
  transition: all 0.2s ease;
  /* Better: only transition what changes */
  transition: background-color 0.2s, transform 0.15s;
}}
.btn:hover {{ background: blue; transform: translateY(-2px); }}

/* Keyframe animations */
@keyframes slide-in {{
  from {{ transform: translateX(-100%); opacity: 0; }}
  to {{ transform: translateX(0); opacity: 1; }}
}}
.panel {{ animation: slide-in 0.3s ease-out forwards; }}

/* View Transitions API (page transitions) */
::view-transition-old(root) {{ animation: fade-out 0.2s; }}
::view-transition-new(root) {{ animation: fade-in 0.2s; }}

/* Scroll-driven animations (no JS!) */
@keyframes reveal {{ from {{ opacity: 0; }} to {{ opacity: 1; }} }}
.card {{
  animation: reveal linear;
  animation-timeline: view();
  animation-range: entry 0% cover 40%;
}}

/* Respect user preferences */
@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) {{
  *, *::before, *::after {{ 
    animation-duration: 0.01ms !important;
    transition-duration: 0.01ms !important;
  }}
}}

cards.title: Responsive Design — Complete Toolkit
cards.contentHtml:
/* Mobile-first media queries */
.grid {{ display: grid; grid-template-columns: 1fr; }}

@media (min-width: 640px) {{ /* sm */
  .grid {{ grid-template-columns: repeat(2, 1fr); }}
}}
@media (min-width: 1024px) {{ /* lg */
  .grid {{ grid-template-columns: repeat(3, 1fr); }}
}}

/* Modern: no breakpoints needed */
.grid {{ grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fit, minmax(min(300px, 100%), 1fr)); }}

/* Fluid typography (clamp) */
h1 {{ font-size: clamp(1.5rem, 4vw, 3rem); }}
/* min: 1.5rem, preferred: 4vw, max: 3rem */

/* Logical properties (LTR/RTL support) */
.card {{
  margin-inline: auto;        /* margin-left + margin-right */
  padding-block: 1rem;        /* padding-top + padding-bottom */
  border-inline-start: 3px solid blue; /* left in LTR, right in RTL */
  inset-inline-start: 0;     /* left in LTR, right in RTL */
}}

sections.intro: CSS Anchor Positioning, View Transitions, Cascade Layers, and the future of native layout.

cards.title: Anchor Positioning API
cards.contentHtml:
Position elements relative to any other "anchor" without shared parents or JS libraries:

.anchor { anchor-name: --my-btn; }

.popover {
  position: absolute;
  position-anchor: --my-btn;
  bottom: anchor(top);
  left: anchor(center);
}
  • Native Popovers: Combined with `popover` attribute for top-layer rendering.
  • Fallback Logic: Use `position-try-options` for safe repositioning.

cards.title: View Transitions API
cards.contentHtml:
Seamlessly animate between DOM states (or pages) without complex JS libraries:

function updateUI() {
  document.startViewTransition(() => {
    // Perform DOM modifications
    renderNewPage(); 
  });
}
  • Pseudo-elements: Automatically creates snapshots `::view-transition-group`.
  • Cross-Document: Supported in 2024/2025 for standard multi-page sites.

cards.title: Cascade Layers (@layer)
cards.contentHtml:
Architectural control over specificity, solving the 3rd-party override library wars:

@layer base, utility, components;

@layer utility {
  .p-10 { padding: 10px !important; }
}

@layer base {
  /* Won't be overridden by higher layers 
     despite specificity */
}

cards.title: Relative Color Syntax (RCS)
cards.contentHtml:
Derive dynamic colors natively from existing variables:

:root { --primary: #3498db; }

.btn {
  /* Extract R, G, B and add 50% alpha */
  background: rgb(from var(--primary) r g b / 0.5);
  /* Or oklch adjustments */
  border-color: oklch(from var(--primary) calc(l + 0.1) c h);
}

sections.intro: Advanced techniques, lesser-known properties, and real-world CSS solutions.

cards.title: Modern CSS Reset (2026)
cards.contentHtml:
/* Modern CSS reset — the essential lines */
*, *::before, *::after {{
  box-sizing: border-box;
  margin: 0;
}}

html {{
  -webkit-text-size-adjust: none;  /* prevent iOS text inflation */
  color-scheme: light dark;         /* support system theme */
}}

body {{
  min-height: 100dvh;              /* dynamic viewport height */
  line-height: 1.6;
  -webkit-font-smoothing: antialiased;
}}

img, picture, video, canvas, svg {{
  display: block;
  max-width: 100%;
}}

input, button, textarea, select {{
  font: inherit;                    /* inherit parent font */
}}

p, h1, h2, h3, h4, h5, h6 {{
  overflow-wrap: break-word;        /* prevent text overflow */
}}

h1, h2, h3, h4 {{ text-wrap: balance; }}  /* balanced line wrapping */
p {{ text-wrap: pretty; }}                 /* prevent orphans */

cards.title: CSS Functions You Should Know
cards.contentHtml:
/* clamp(min, preferred, max) — responsive values */
font-size: clamp(1rem, 2.5vw, 2rem);
padding: clamp(1rem, 3vw, 3rem);

/* min() / max() */
width: min(100%, 600px);   /* max-width without media query */
padding: max(1rem, 2vw);   /* minimum padding guarantee */

/* calc() — mix units */
width: calc(100% - 2rem);
height: calc(100vh - var(--header-height));
grid-template-columns: calc(50% - 0.5rem) calc(50% - 0.5rem);

/* color-mix() — blend colors at runtime */
background: color-mix(in srgb, var(--primary) 20%, transparent);
/* Creates 20% opacity primary without rgba */

/* light-dark() — theme-aware values */
color: light-dark(#333, #eee);  /* auto switches per color-scheme */

/* round() — round values to a grid */
width: round(100% / 3, 1px);  /* prevent subpixel issues */

cards.title: Scroll Snap — Native Carousels
cards.contentHtml:
/* Container */
.carousel {{
  display: flex;
  overflow-x: auto;
  scroll-snap-type: x mandatory;  /* snap horizontally */
  scroll-behavior: smooth;
  scrollbar-width: none;          /* hide scrollbar (Firefox) */
}}
.carousel::-webkit-scrollbar {{ display: none; }}

/* Items */
.carousel-item {{
  flex: 0 0 100%;               /* full width per item */
  scroll-snap-align: start;       /* snap to start of item */
}}

/* Partial visibility (show peek of next) */
.carousel-item {{ flex: 0 0 85%; }}
.carousel {{ gap: 1rem; padding-inline: 1rem; }}

/* Vertical snap */
.fullpage {{ scroll-snap-type: y mandatory; height: 100dvh; overflow-y: auto; }}
.fullpage section {{ height: 100dvh; scroll-snap-align: start; }}

cards.title: CSS Shapes & Clip Paths
cards.contentHtml:
/* clip-path: create non-rectangular elements */
.triangle {{ clip-path: polygon(50% 0%, 0% 100%, 100% 100%); }}
.circle {{ clip-path: circle(50%); }}
.diamond {{ clip-path: polygon(50% 0, 100% 50%, 50% 100%, 0 50%); }}

/* Blob/organic shape */
.blob {{ border-radius: 30% 70% 70% 30% / 30% 30% 70% 70%; }}

/* shape-outside: text wraps around shape */
.avatar {{
  float: left;
  shape-outside: circle(50%);
  clip-path: circle(50%);
  width: 150px;
  margin-right: 1rem;
}}

cards.title: accent-color, color-scheme & System Integration
cards.contentHtml:
/* accent-color: theme form controls with one line */
:root {{ accent-color: #6366f1; }}
/* Applies to: checkbox, radio, range, progress */
/* Automatic contrast text color for checked state */

/* color-scheme: tell browser about supported themes */
:root {{ color-scheme: light dark; }}
/* Browser auto-styles: scrollbars, form controls, */
/* default backgrounds and text colors per theme */

/* @media queries for user preferences */
@media (prefers-color-scheme: dark) {{ }}
@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) {{ }}
@media (prefers-contrast: more) {{ }}
@media (forced-colors: active) {{ /* Windows High Contrast */ }}
@media (prefers-reduced-data: reduce) {{ /* limit data usage */ }}
@media (scripting: none) {{ /* JS disabled */ }}
accent-color is the easiest design win in CSS — one line colors ALL native form controls to match your brand. No more ugly default blue checkboxes.

sections.intro: CSS architecture, methodologies, performance, and the modern tooling landscape.

cards.title: CSS Specificity — The Rules
cards.contentHtml:
/* Specificity: (inline, IDs, classes, elements) */
*                    /* 0,0,0,0 */
p                    /* 0,0,0,1 */
.class               /* 0,0,1,0 */
#id                  /* 0,1,0,0 */
style="..."          /* 1,0,0,0 */
!important           /* overrides everything (avoid!) */

p.intro              /* 0,0,1,1 (element + class) */
#nav .link:hover     /* 0,1,2,0 (ID + class + pseudo-class) */

/* :where() has ZERO specificity */
:where(.card) p {{ }} /* 0,0,0,1 (only p counts) */

/* :is() takes HIGHEST specificity of its arguments */
:is(#id, .class) p {{ }} /* 0,1,0,1 (#id wins) */

/* @layer lowers entire layer priority */
@layer base {{ #id {{ color: red; }} }} /* loses to un-layered .class */
Specificity is NOT a single number. It's a tuple: (inline, IDs, classes, elements). (0,1,0,0) ALWAYS beats (0,0,99,99) — one ID beats 99 classes. This is why !important and inline styles are hard to override.

cards.title: CSS Architecture & Methodologies
cards.contentHtml:
BEM (Block Element Modifier)

/* Block: standalone component */
.card {{ }}
/* Element: part of block (double underscore) */
.card__title {{ }}
.card__body {{ }}
/* Modifier: variation (double dash) */
.card--featured {{ }}
.card__title--large {{ }}

Modern Approaches

CSS Modules: .module.css files with locally-scoped classes (auto-generated unique names). Tailwind CSS: Utility-first — class="flex items-center gap-4 p-6 rounded-lg". CSS-in-JS: styled-components, Emotion — co-locate styles with components. Vanilla Extract: Type-safe, zero-runtime CSS-in-TS. Open Props: Design tokens as custom properties.

In 2026, Tailwind CSS is the dominant approach for React/Next.js apps. CSS Modules are the safe, framework-agnostic option. CSS-in-JS (styled-components) has fallen out of favor due to runtime overhead and RSC incompatibility.

cards.title: Performance Optimization
cards.contentHtml:
Critical Rendering Path

HTML parsing → DOM tree + CSSOM → Render tree → Layout → Paint → Composite. CSS blocks rendering — the browser won't paint until ALL CSS is parsed. JS blocks HTML parsing (unless defer/async).

Optimization Techniques

Critical CSS: Inline above-fold styles in <head>, lazy-load the rest. content-visibility: auto: Skip rendering offscreen content. contain: Isolate layout/paint/style recalculation. will-change: Hint browser about upcoming animations (use sparingly). Avoid layout thrashing: batch reads before writes. Prefer transform/opacity: Only these two properties are GPU-composited — avoid animating width/height/top/left.

/* content-visibility: skip rendering offscreen sections */
.section {{ content-visibility: auto; contain-intrinsic-size: auto 500px; }}

/* contain: isolate recalculation */
.card {{ contain: layout style paint; }}

/* Only animate compositable properties */
.animate {{ 
  transform: translateX(0);  /* ✅ GPU composited */
  opacity: 1;                /* ✅ GPU composited */
  /* ❌ Avoid: width, height, top, left, margin, padding */
}}

sections.intro: Recent CSS evolutions drastically reduce the need for preprocessors and complex JS logic.

cards.title: Game-Changing Selectors
cards.contentHtml:
• :has() (The Parent Selector): Selects an element if it contains a certain descendent. Ex: `figure:has(figcaption)` selects only figures that have captions. It completely changes CSS architecture.
  • :is() and :where(): Groups selectors succinctly. `:is(h1, h2, h3) span` instead of writing them all out. `:where()` is identical but has zero specificity, perfect for library resets.
  • Native Nesting: CSS now natively supports standard nesting (similar to SCSS/LESS) without build tools. `nav { ul { padding: 0; } }`

cards.title: Layout & Responsiveness
cards.contentHtml:
• Container Queries (@container): Instead of styling based on viewport width (`@media`), style based on the parent wrapper's width. This allows reusable components (like cards) to look totally different inside a tight sidebar vs a massive main grid.
  • CSS Subgrid: Allows nested elements to perfectly align to their parent's grid tracks. If a Card has a Grid layout, `grid-template-rows: subgrid` allows headers and text across multiple sibling cards to perfectly align with each other.
  • text-wrap: balance / pretty: Improves typography natively. `balance` prevents massive headlines with a single "widow" word on the final line by mathematically balancing the text block width.

sections.intro: Building the web for everyone. Accessibility is not an afterthought; it is fundamental to modern markup.

cards.title: Semantic HTML Boundaries
cards.contentHtml:
Semantic HTML natively provides meaning and keyboard functionality that plain `<div>`s lack.

  • <main>: The dominant content of the page. Only one visible per document.
  • <article> vs <section>: An `article` is independently distributable (a blog post). A `section` groups related content structurally but isn't necessarily standalone.
  • <nav>: Wrap major navigation blocks. Provide an `aria-label` like "Main" or "Footer" if multiple exist.
  • <button> vs <a>: `<a href>` navigates. `<button>` performs an action on the page. Never use `<div onClick>` without adding `tabindex="0"`, `role="button"`, and handling the `Enter/Space` keys manually.

cards.title: ARIA (Accessible Rich Internet Applications)
cards.contentHtml:
ARIA bridges the gap for complex interactive UI (tabs, modals, comboboxes) where native HTML falls short.

  • First Rule of ARIA: No ARIA is better than bad ARIA. Native elements are always preferred.
  • aria-hidden="true": Hides decorative elements (like SVG icons) from screen readers.
  • aria-label / aria-labelledby: Provides an invisible text label for icon-only buttons (like a search magnifying glass).
  • aria-live: Used on dynamically updated areas (e.g. error messages, toast notifications). Screen readers will interrupt and read `"assertive"` updates immediately, or wait for pauses for `"polite"` updates.

sections.intro: Core Web Vitals (INP, LCP, CLS), Resource Prioritization, and Interaction to Next Paint.

cards.title: Interaction to Next Paint (INP)
cards.contentHtml:
The modern metric representing total interaction latency. INP tracks every click, tap, and keyboard interaction throughout the entire session:

  • Input Delay: Time between user interaction and event handler start (minimize main thread blocking).
  • Processing Time: Time spent inside the event handler (optimize logic, use `setTimeout(0)` or `isInputPending`).
  • Presentation Delay: Time spent painting the frame (avoid layout thrashing / heavy CSS).

cards.title: Resource Prioritization
cards.contentHtml:
Telling the browser what strictly matters for the Critical Rendering Path:


<img src="hero.avif" 
  fetchpriority="high" 
  loading="eager" 
  decoding="async" 
  alt="LCP Hero">


<script type="speculationrules">
{
  "prerender": [
    { "source": "list", "urls": ["/next-page"] }
  ]
}
</script>

cards.title: Image & Asset Optimization
cards.contentHtml:
Modern formats and strategies for minimizing cumulative layout shifts:

  • AVIF & WebP: Native compression over 50% better than JPEG.
  • Aspect Ratio: Reserve space before images load to prevent layout shifts.
  • Source Set: Use `srcset` and `sizes` to deliver the perfect pixel density for the device.

cards.title: Core Web Vital Thresholds
cards.contentHtml:
Metric Good Poor
LCP (Paint) < 2.5s > 4.0s
INP (Latency) < 200ms > 500ms
CLS (Shift) < 0.1 > 0.25

sections.intro: Must-know CSS properties organized by category — the complete reference for frontend developers.

cards.title: Typography — Every Property
cards.contentHtml:
font-family: "Inter", system-ui, sans-serif;
font-size: 1rem;             /* prefer rem over px */
font-weight: 400;            /* 100-900, or normal/bold */
font-style: italic;
font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums; /* aligned numbers */
line-height: 1.6;            /* unitless = multiplier (best) */
letter-spacing: -0.02em;     /* tighten headings */
word-spacing: 0.05em;
text-align: left | center | right | justify;
text-decoration: underline wavy red;
text-transform: uppercase | lowercase | capitalize;
text-indent: 2rem;           /* first line indent */
text-overflow: ellipsis;     /* needs overflow:hidden + nowrap */
text-wrap: balance;          /* even line distribution (headings) */
text-wrap: pretty;           /* prevent orphans (paragraphs) */
white-space: nowrap | pre | pre-wrap | pre-line;
word-break: break-all;       /* break ANY word */
overflow-wrap: break-word;   /* break only when needed */
hyphens: auto;               /* auto-hyphenation (needs lang=) */
-webkit-line-clamp: 3;       /* multi-line truncation */
font-variant-ligatures: none; /* disable ligatures in code */
font-display: swap;          /* font loading strategy */
unicode-range: U+0000-00FF;  /* subset font characters */

cards.title: Colors, Backgrounds & Borders
cards.contentHtml:
/* COLORS (modern) */
color: #333;
color: rgb(51 51 51);         /* modern syntax (no commas) */
color: rgb(51 51 51 / 0.5);   /* with alpha */
color: hsl(220 50% 50%);
color: oklch(70% 0.15 250);   /* perceptually uniform */
color: color(display-p3 1 0 0); /* wide gamut */
color: color-mix(in oklch, var(--primary) 20%, transparent);
color: light-dark(#333, #eee); /* auto theme switching */

/* BACKGROUNDS */
background-color: #f0f0f0;
background-image: url("bg.jpg");
background: linear-gradient(135deg, #667eea, #764ba2);
background: radial-gradient(circle at top, #fff, #000);
background: conic-gradient(from 45deg, red, blue, red);
background-size: cover | contain | 100px auto;
background-position: center | top right;
background-repeat: no-repeat | repeat-x | space;
background-attachment: scroll | fixed | local;
background-clip: border-box | padding-box | text;
background-blend-mode: multiply | overlay | screen;

/* BORDERS */
border: 1px solid #ddd;
border-radius: 8px;           /* or 50% for circle */
border-image: linear-gradient(red, blue) 1;
outline: 2px solid blue;
outline-offset: 3px;          /* gap between element and outline */

/* SHADOWS */
box-shadow: 0 4px 12px rgb(0 0 0 / 0.1);
box-shadow: inset 0 2px 4px rgb(0 0 0 / 0.1); /* inner */
filter: drop-shadow(0 4px 8px rgb(0 0 0 / 0.2)); /* follows shape */

cards.title: Transforms, Filters & Visual Effects
cards.contentHtml:
/* TRANSFORMS (GPU composited — FAST) */
transform: translateX(50px) translateY(-10px);
transform: translate3d(0, 0, 0);  /* force GPU layer */
transform: scale(1.1);
transform: rotate(45deg);
transform: skewX(10deg);
transform-origin: top left;

/* FILTERS */
filter: blur(4px);
filter: brightness(1.2) contrast(1.1);
filter: grayscale(1);           /* black and white */
filter: sepia(0.5);             /* vintage effect */
filter: saturate(1.5);          /* boost colors */
filter: hue-rotate(90deg);
filter: invert(1);              /* invert colors */

/* BACKDROP (frosted glass) */
backdrop-filter: blur(16px) saturate(180%);

/* BLEND MODES */
mix-blend-mode: multiply | screen | overlay | difference;

/* CLIP & MASK */
clip-path: circle(50%);
clip-path: polygon(50% 0%, 100% 100%, 0% 100%);
mask-image: linear-gradient(black, transparent);

/* TRANSITIONS */
transition: all 0.2s ease;
transition: background-color 0.2s, transform 0.15s ease-out;
transition-behavior: allow-discrete;  /* transition display */

/* ANIMATIONS */
animation: slide-in 0.3s ease-out forwards;
animation-timeline: scroll();   /* scroll-driven */
animation-timeline: view();     /* visibility-driven */
For animations, ONLY use transform and opacity — they run on the GPU compositor thread and don't trigger layout or paint. Animating width, height, top, or left causes expensive layout recalculations on every frame.

cards.title: Layout, Sizing & Overflow
cards.contentHtml:
/* SIZING */
width: 100% | fit-content | min-content | max-content;
max-width: 1200px;
min-width: 0;                /* important for flex overflow! */
height: auto;                /* prefer auto or min-height */
min-height: 100dvh;          /* dynamic viewport height */
aspect-ratio: 16 / 9;

/* OBJECT (for img/video) */
object-fit: cover | contain | fill | none;
object-position: center top;

/* OVERFLOW */
overflow: hidden | auto | scroll | clip;
overflow-x: auto; overflow-y: hidden;
overscroll-behavior: contain;  /* prevent scroll chaining */

/* SCROLL */
scroll-behavior: smooth;
scroll-padding-top: 80px;     /* offset for sticky header */
scroll-snap-type: x mandatory;
scroll-snap-align: start;
scrollbar-width: thin | none;
scrollbar-color: #888 transparent;

/* DISPLAY */
display: block | inline | inline-block | flex | grid | none;
display: contents;            /* element disappears, children stay */
visibility: hidden;           /* invisible but keeps space */

/* POSITION */
position: static | relative | absolute | fixed | sticky;
inset: 0;                    /* shorthand: top+right+bottom+left */
z-index: 10;                 /* only on positioned/flex/grid */

/* SYSTEM */
accent-color: #6366f1;       /* theme native form controls */
color-scheme: light dark;     /* support system themes */
cursor: pointer | not-allowed | grab;
pointer-events: none;         /* click-through */
user-select: none;            /* prevent text selection */
resize: both | vertical;      /* resizable element */
content-visibility: auto;     /* skip offscreen rendering */
contain: layout paint;        /* isolate recalculation */
will-change: transform;       /* GPU optimization hint */
isolation: isolate;           /* new stacking context */

sections.intro: 60 interview questions from very basic to very advanced. Click to reveal answers.

qa.question: What does CSS stand for?
qa.answerHtml: Cascading Style Sheets.

qa.question: Explain the CSS Box Model.
qa.answerHtml: Every element is a rectangular box composed of four areas: Content (the actual stuff), Padding (transparent space around content), Border (around the padding), and Margin (transparent space outside the border).

qa.question: What is a CSS selector?
qa.answerHtml: The part of a CSS rule that targets which HTML elements to style (e.g., class `.btn`, ID `#header`, attribute `[disabled]`).

qa.question: What is the primary difference between CSS Grid and Flexbox?
qa.answerHtml: Flexbox is designed for 1-dimensional layouts (working either in a row OR a column). CSS Grid is designed for 2-dimensional layouts (managing rows AND columns simultaneously).

qa.question: What is the cascading aspect of CSS?
qa.answerHtml: If two different rules apply to the same element, the cascade determines which one wins based on Source Order, Specificity, and Importance (`!important`).

qa.question: How does CSS Specificity work?
qa.answerHtml: It's calculated mathematically. Inline styles > IDs > Classes/Attributes/Pseudo-classes > Elements/Pseudo-elements. E.g., `#nav .btn` beats `div div .btn`.

qa.question: What are CSS Custom Properties (Variables)?
qa.answerHtml: Defined as `--primary: red;`, used as `color: var(--primary)`. Unlike SCSS variables (which compile out statically), CSS variables live entirely in the DOM natively. They cascade, and JS can alter them dynamically.

qa.question: Explain the CSS `:has()` selector.
qa.answerHtml: The long-awaited "parent selector". `a:has(> img)` strictly targets anchor tags that contain direct child images. It eliminates the need for massive amounts of JS DOM manipulation.

qa.question: What are CSS Container Queries?
qa.answerHtml: Instead of media queries targeting the viewport width, container queries (`@container`) target the parent element's width. A card component can radically re-layout itself depending on whether it's embedded in a narrow sidebar or a wide grid, totally redefining component reusability.

qa.question: What is `CSS Subgrid`?
qa.answerHtml: Allows a nested grid container to inherit its parent's grid tracks instead of defining its own. Essential for perfectly aligning nested headers across adjacent sibling cards.

qa.question: What is hardware acceleration in CSS?
qa.answerHtml: Properties like `transform` and `opacity` are completely offloaded from the main CPU thread to the GPU. This means animations using these properties run flawlessly at 60fps without triggering layout paints.

qa.question: How do you create a triangle natively in pure CSS?
qa.answerHtml: By setting an element to `width: 0; height: 0;` and using thick borders on adjacent sides, with one side colored and the perpendicular sides transparent.

qa.question: What does `minmax()` do in CSS Grid?
qa.answerHtml: Defines a track's minimum and maximum size constraint. Extensively used in auto-responsive grids: `grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fit, minmax(200px, 1fr));`.

qa.question: What is the utility-first CSS approach?
qa.answerHtml: The philosophy popularized by Tailwind CSS. Instead of writing semantic classes `.btn-primary` with custom CSS, you apply tiny, single-function utility classes directly in markup: `bg-blue-500 px-4 py-2 rounded`. It massively increases speed and eliminates dead CSS.

qa.question: What are CSS `scroll-timeline` animations?
qa.answerHtml: A modern feature allowing CSS animations to be directly bonded to the user's scroll depth instead of time, enabling totally native parallax effects without JavaScript.

qa.question: What is CSS Anchor Positioning?
qa.answerHtml: A new API allowing an element to be positioned relative to one or more "anchor" elements on the page, without requiring a shared parent stacking context. It replaces massive amounts of JS positioning libraries (like Floating UI) for tooltips, menus, and popovers.

qa.question: Explain "Relative Color Syntax" in CSS.
qa.answerHtml: A feature allowing you to derive a new color from an existing one using the `from` keyword: `color: rgb(from var(--bg) r g b / 0.5)`. This enables dynamic transparency and lighting adjustments without needing Preprocessors or dozens of variables.

qa.question: What are CSS "Cascade Layers" (@layer)?
qa.answerHtml: A system to explicitly control the order of the cascade, regardless of specificity. Layers like `base`, `utils`, and `components` can be prioritized so that even high-specificity library styles can be overridden by low-specificity custom utilities.

qa.question: Explain CSS "Nesting" (Native).
qa.answerHtml: Native browser support for nesting selectors like `& {}`, similar to Sass. It reduces code repetition and improves readability, and unlike preprocessors, it lives entirely in the browser, reducing build step complexity.

qa.question: What is "Wide Gamut" color in CSS (oklch, lab)?
qa.answerHtml: Modern color spaces that can represent 50% more colors than standard sRGB. `oklch` is particularly powerful as it organizes color by perceived Lightness, Chroma, and Hue, making accessibility calculations much more intuitive.

qa.question: What is its "Scope" in CSS (@scope)?
qa.answerHtml: A rule that allows scoping styles to a specific subtree of the DOM, optionally with "donut holes" (limits). It provides true native encapsulation for components without requiring Shadow DOM.

qa.question: What is "CSS Masonry" (Proposed)?
qa.answerHtml: A native extension to CSS Grid (`grid-template-rows: masonry`) that automatically packs items into the "shortest" column, creating Pinterest-style layouts natively without JavaScript.

qa.question: What is `image-set()` in modern CSS?
qa.answerHtml: An upgraded version of `srcset` for CSS backgrounds. It allows providing multiple image formats (like avif, webp, png) and resolutions, and the browser automatically picks the best one based on support and DPI.

qa.question: What is "CSS Motion Path" (`offset-path`)?
qa.answerHtml: Allows an element to follow a custom path (like an SVG path) during animation. You can control its progress along the path using `offset-distance` and its orientation using `offset-rotate`.

qa.question: How does "Tree Shaking" impact CSS performance?
qa.answerHtml: Using tools like PurgeCSS or Tailwinds JIT to scan your HTML/JS and remove any CSS selectors that aren't being used, resulting in significantly smaller CSS bundles and faster download/parsing.

qa.question: What is "Critical CSS" inlining?
qa.answerHtml: The practice of extracting the CSS required ONLY for the above-the-fold content and inlining it directly into the ``. This eliminates the blocking network request for the main CSS file for initial paint.

qa.question: How does "CSS Containment" (`contain`) help?
qa.answerHtml: It allows developers to isolate parts of a page from the rest of the layout, paint, or size calculations. The browser can then skip re-calculating the entire page if something changes inside a "contained" element.

qa.question: Explain "Referrer Policy" in CSS and HTML.
qa.answerHtml: Controls how much referrer information (the URL the user came from) is sent with requests. `strict-origin-when-cross-origin` is the modern secure default.

qa.question: Explain "CSS Shapes" (`shape-outside`).
qa.answerHtml: Allows text to wrap around complex shapes (circles, polygons) defined for a floated element, going beyond simple rectangular wrap behavior.

qa.question: What is the "Columns" property in CSS Layout?
qa.answerHtml: A native multi-column layout system (like a newspaper) that automatically flows content across columns. `column-count: 3; column-gap: 20px;`.

qa.question: Explain "Atomic CSS" methodology.
qa.answerHtml: Building UIs by composing small, single-purpose classes. While it results in more classes in HTML, it minimizes total CSS volume as classes are reused thousands of times across a project.

qa.question: What is the CSS box model?
qa.answerHtml: Every element is a rectangular box with: content (inner area) → padding (space inside border) → border → margin (space outside border). box-sizing: border-box makes width/height include padding+border (much more intuitive than the default content-box).

qa.question: What is the difference between margin and padding?
qa.answerHtml: Padding: space INSIDE the border (between content and border). Affected by background-color. Margin: space OUTSIDE the border (between this element and others). Always transparent. Margins collapse vertically (larger wins); padding never collapses.

qa.question: What is Flexbox?
qa.answerHtml: One-dimensional layout system (row OR column). Container: display: flex. Key properties: justify-content (main axis alignment), align-items (cross axis), flex-wrap, gap. Items: flex-grow/shrink/basis, align-self, order. Use for: navbars, card rows, centering, equal-height columns.

qa.question: What is CSS Grid?
qa.answerHtml: Two-dimensional layout system (rows AND columns simultaneously). display: grid. Key: grid-template-columns/rows, gap, grid-template-areas. Items can span rows/columns. repeat(auto-fill, minmax(250px, 1fr)) for responsive layouts. Use for: page layouts, dashboards, card grids, any 2D arrangement.

qa.question: What is the difference between Flexbox and Grid?
qa.answerHtml: Flexbox: 1D (row OR column), content-driven sizing, items control their own size. Best for: components, navbars, alignment. Grid: 2D (rows AND columns), layout-driven, container controls item placement. Best for: page layouts, dashboards. Use both together — Grid for page structure, Flexbox for component internals.

qa.question: What is CSS specificity?
qa.answerHtml: How browsers decide which styles win when multiple rules target the same element. Specificity tuple: (inline, IDs, classes, elements). p = 0,0,0,1. .class = 0,0,1,0. #id = 0,1,0,0. An ID always beats any number of classes. !important overrides everything (avoid it).

qa.question: What are CSS selectors?
qa.answerHtml: Patterns to target elements. Element: p. Class: .btn. ID: #nav. Descendant: div p. Child: div > p. Adjacent sibling: h2 + p. General sibling: h2 ~ p. Attribute: [type='text']. Pseudo-class: :hover, :first-child, :nth-child(). Pseudo-element: ::before, ::after.

qa.question: What is the position property?
qa.answerHtml: static: default, normal flow. relative: offset from normal position, creates stacking context. absolute: removed from flow, positioned relative to nearest positioned ancestor. fixed: relative to viewport (stays on scroll). sticky: normal until scroll threshold, then fixed.

qa.question: What is z-index?
qa.answerHtml: Controls stacking order of positioned elements (not static). Higher z-index = on top. Only works on positioned elements (relative, absolute, fixed, sticky) and flex/grid children. Creates stacking contexts. Child z-index is relative to parent stacking context — a child can never escape its parent's context.

qa.question: What are pseudo-elements?
qa.answerHtml: ::before and ::after insert virtual elements as first/last child. Require content property. ::placeholder styles input placeholders. ::selection styles selected text. ::first-line/::first-letter for typography. ::backdrop for dialog/fullscreen overlays. Double colon (::) is correct syntax.

qa.question: What is the display property?
qa.answerHtml: block: full width, new line. inline: no width/height, stays in flow. inline-block: inline but accepts dimensions. none: removed from layout (use visibility: hidden to hide but keep space). flex: flex container. grid: grid container. contents: element disappears, children move up.

qa.question: Explain CSS custom properties (variables).
qa.answerHtml: Defined with --name: value, used with var(--name, fallback). Cascade and inherit. Scope: :root for global, any selector for scoped. Can be changed dynamically with JS: element.style.setProperty('--color', 'red'). Enable theming, dynamic values, and reduce repetition. Unlike Sass variables, they're runtime values.

qa.question: What are container queries?
qa.answerHtml: Style elements based on their CONTAINER's size, not the viewport. container-type: inline-size on parent, then @container (min-width: 400px) { } for children. Revolutionary for reusable components — a card adapts whether it's in a sidebar or main content. Container query units: cqi, cqw, cqh.

qa.question: What is the :has() selector?
qa.answerHtml: The 'parent selector' — styles an element based on its children/siblings. .card:has(img) targets cards containing images. form:has(:invalid) .submit { opacity: 0.5 } dims submit if form has errors. h2:has(+ p) targets h2 followed by p. The most powerful CSS selector ever added — many things that required JS are now CSS-only.

qa.question: Explain CSS nesting.
qa.answerHtml: Native CSS now supports nesting (like Sass): .card { color: black; &:hover { color: blue; } & .title { font-size: 1.5rem; } @media (width >= 768px) { padding: 2rem; } }. The & represents the parent selector. Reduces repetition, improves readability. Supported in all modern browsers since 2023.

qa.question: What is the @layer at-rule?
qa.answerHtml: Controls cascade order explicitly: @layer base, components, utilities;. Later layers win over earlier ones, regardless of specificity within layers. Un-layered styles beat ALL layers. Use for: managing CSS framework overrides, organizing large codebases, ensuring utility classes always win (Tailwind uses this).

qa.question: What is BEM methodology?
qa.answerHtml: Block Element Modifier: .block__element--modifier. Block: standalone component (.card). Element: part of block (.card__title). Modifier: variation (.card--featured). Benefits: predictable naming, flat specificity, clear relationships. Drawback: verbose class names. Still widely used in non-utility-first codebases.

qa.question: What is margin collapsing?
qa.answerHtml: Adjacent vertical margins collapse — only the larger one applies. margin-bottom: 20px + margin-top: 30px = 30px gap (not 50px). Happens: between siblings, parent-child (when no padding/border separates them), empty elements. Does NOT happen: with flex/grid items, floated elements, absolutely positioned elements, or elements with overflow != visible.

qa.question: What is the difference between em, rem, px, %, vh/vw?
qa.answerHtml: px: absolute (1 CSS pixel). em: relative to parent font-size (compounds!). rem: relative to root font-size (predictable). %: relative to parent's property. vh/vw: viewport height/width. dvh/dvw: dynamic viewport (accounts for mobile URL bar). cqi: container query inline size. Use rem for font sizes, px for borders, % or fr for layout.

qa.question: How do you center an element vertically and horizontally?
qa.answerHtml: Flex: display: flex; justify-content: center; align-items: center;. Grid: display: grid; place-items: center; (shortest). Position: position: absolute; inset: 0; margin: auto; (needs defined width/height). Transform: position: absolute; top: 50%; left: 50%; transform: translate(-50%, -50%);.

qa.question: What is content-visibility?
qa.answerHtml: content-visibility: auto skips rendering of offscreen elements. The browser only computes layout/paint for visible elements. contain-intrinsic-size: auto 500px provides estimated size to prevent scroll jumps. Can improve initial render time by 50%+ on long pages. Think of it as 'virtualization for CSS.'

qa.question: Explain stacking contexts in depth.
qa.answerHtml: A stacking context is a 3D conceptualization where elements have z-axis position. Created by: positioned elements with z-index, opacity < 1, transform/filter/clip-path, flex/grid children with z-index, isolation: isolate. Key rule: z-index is RELATIVE to the stacking context — a child cannot appear above a sibling of its parent's stacking context, regardless of z-index value. This is why z-index 'doesn't work' sometimes.

qa.question: Explain the CSS contain property.
qa.answerHtml: contain: layout | paint | size | style. Tells browser that this element is isolated — changes inside don't affect outside. layout: layout of children doesn't affect ancestors. paint: content doesn't paint outside bounds. size: element's size isn't affected by children. content = layout paint style. strict = all. Enables browser to skip work during recalculation. Foundation of content-visibility.

qa.question: What is the CSS cascade in detail?
qa.answerHtml: Order of priority (highest to lowest): 1) User agent !important. 2) User !important. 3) Author !important. 4) Author styles (normal). 5) User styles (normal). 6) User agent styles. Within same origin: a) @layer order (un-layered > layered). b) Specificity. c) Source order (last wins). The cascade is more nuanced than most developers realize — @layer fundamentally changed it.

qa.question: How do CSS animations perform vs JavaScript animations?
qa.answerHtml: CSS transform and opacity animations run on the GPU compositor thread — they don't trigger layout/paint and don't block the main thread. CSS width/height/top animations trigger layout (expensive). JS animations via requestAnimationFrame run on main thread — can be janky if main thread is busy. Web Animations API (element.animate()) can be compositor-promoted like CSS. For best performance: use CSS transforms, avoid layout-triggering properties.

qa.question: What is subgrid?
qa.answerHtml: grid-template-columns: subgrid on a grid child makes it inherit parent's column/row tracks. Solves: aligning nested grids with parent (card content aligning across cards). Without subgrid, each card has independent internal grid — headers/footers may not align. With subgrid, they share the same grid lines. Supported in all modern browsers since 2023.

qa.question: What are scroll-driven animations?
qa.answerHtml: CSS animations linked to scroll position instead of time. animation-timeline: scroll() for scroll progress, animation-timeline: view() for element visibility. animation-range: entry 0% cover 40% controls when animation plays. No JavaScript needed. Use for: reveal-on-scroll, parallax, progress indicators. Spec includes ScrollTimeline and ViewTimeline JS APIs for complex cases.

qa.question: Explain the will-change property.
qa.answerHtml: will-change: transform hints the browser to optimize for upcoming changes — creates a new compositor layer, allocates GPU memory. Improves animation performance BUT: uses extra memory, creates stacking context, should be added just before animation and removed after. Don't apply globally (* { will-change: transform }) — it wastes resources. Use sparingly, remove when done.

qa.question: How would you architect CSS for a large application?
qa.answerHtml: Design tokens: CSS custom properties for colors, spacing, typography, shadows. Reset/base: Modern reset (Tailwind preflight or custom). Layout: CSS Grid for page, Flexbox for components. Components: CSS Modules or Tailwind for scoping. Utilities: Tailwind or custom utility classes in highest @layer. Theming: CSS variables + prefers-color-scheme + data-theme attribute. Performance: Critical CSS inlined, rest lazy-loaded, content-visibility for long pages. Organization: @layer for cascade control, logical grouping.

qa.question: What is the cascade in CSS?
qa.answerHtml: The algorithm that determines which styles apply when multiple rules target the same element. Order: 1) Importance (!important). 2) Origin (author > user > browser). 3) Layer order (@layer). 4) Specificity. 5) Source order (last wins). Understanding the cascade is the foundation of CSS mastery.

qa.question: What is a CSS reset/normalize?
qa.answerHtml: Reset (Eric Meyer's): strips ALL default browser styles to zero. Normalize (normalize.css): makes defaults consistent across browsers while preserving useful ones. Modern approach: Tailwind Preflight or custom reset with box-sizing: border-box, margin reset, and sensible defaults.

qa.question: What is the difference between visibility: hidden and display: none?
qa.answerHtml: display: none: element is completely removed from layout (no space, no interaction, not accessible). visibility: hidden: element is invisible but still takes up space. Screen readers skip both. For accessible hiding (visible to screen readers only), use the sr-only/visually-hidden class pattern.

qa.question: What is the float property?
qa.answerHtml: Originally for text wrapping around images. float: left|right removes element from normal flow and pushes it to one side. Parent collapses unless cleared (clear: both) or uses overflow: hidden. Don't use for layout — Flexbox and Grid have completely replaced float-based layouts.

qa.question: What are pseudo-classes?
qa.answerHtml: Select elements based on state: :hover (mouse over), :focus (focused), :active (being clicked), :visited (visited link), :first-child, :last-child, :nth-child(2n) (even children), :not(.class), :focus-visible (keyboard focus only), :disabled, :checked, :empty (no children).

qa.question: What is the overflow property?
qa.answerHtml: visible: content overflows (default). hidden: clips overflow, creates BFC. scroll: always shows scrollbar. auto: scrollbar only when needed. clip: like hidden but doesn't create scroll container. Can set per axis: overflow-x: hidden; overflow-y: auto;.

qa.question: What is a Block Formatting Context (BFC)?
qa.answerHtml: A layout context where floats are contained and margins don't collapse through it. Created by: overflow (not visible), display: flow-root (modern, no side effects), float, position: absolute/fixed, flex/grid items. display: flow-root is the intended way to create a BFC.

qa.question: What are logical properties?
qa.answerHtml: Direction-aware alternatives to physical properties: margin-inline-start (left in LTR, right in RTL), padding-block (top + bottom), inset-inline (left + right), border-block-end (bottom). Enable RTL support without separate stylesheets. inline = horizontal, block = vertical (in horizontal writing modes).

qa.question: What is the clamp() function?
qa.answerHtml: clamp(min, preferred, max) — value that scales with preferred but never goes below min or above max. Most common use: font-size: clamp(1rem, 2.5vw, 2rem) — fluid typography that adapts to viewport. Also works for spacing, widths, any length value. Eliminates many media queries.

qa.question: What is the aspect-ratio property?
qa.answerHtml: aspect-ratio: 16 / 9 maintains a ratio regardless of content/container size. Replaces the old padding-bottom hack. Use for: video containers, image placeholders, card layouts. Combined with object-fit: cover for images/video that fill the ratio without distortion.

qa.question: What is object-fit?
qa.answerHtml: Controls how replaced content (img, video) fills its container. cover: fills container, crops if needed (most common). contain: fits inside container, may have gaps. fill: stretches to fill (distorts). none: original size. scale-down: smallest of none or contain. object-position controls alignment.

qa.question: What is the difference between transform and position for moving elements?
qa.answerHtml: transform: translate() moves the element visually without affecting layout — other elements don't reflow. It's GPU-composited (fast). position: relative/absolute with top/left also moves elements but may trigger layout recalculation. For animations, ALWAYS use transform — it's orders of magnitude faster.

qa.question: How do CSS transitions work?
qa.answerHtml: transition: property duration timing-function delay. Animates between two states (e.g., normal → hover). Only works for properties that have calculable intermediate values (not display). transition: all is convenient but less performant — specify exact properties. Timing functions: ease, linear, ease-in-out, or cubic-bezier().

qa.question: What is the gap property?
qa.answerHtml: Sets spacing between flex/grid items without margin on individual items. gap: 1rem (both axes) or row-gap: 1rem; column-gap: 2rem;. Works in Flexbox (since 2020) and Grid. Advantages over margin: no double margins, no negative margins for edge items, simpler responsive adjustments.

qa.question: Explain CSS Paint API (Houdini).
qa.answerHtml: Part of CSS Houdini — allows custom paint worklets: CSS.paintWorklet.addModule('painter.js'). Inside the worklet, you draw using Canvas-like API. Used as: background: paint(my-painter). Enables: custom backgrounds, borders, decorations without images. Still limited browser support but shows the future of CSS extensibility.

qa.question: What is the @scope at-rule?
qa.answerHtml: @scope (.card) to (.card-footer) { p { color: gray; } } — styles only apply between the scoping root (.card) and the limit (.card-footer). Without 'to', scopes to all descendants. Provides true CSS scoping without Shadow DOM or CSS Modules. Allows donut scoping — style content between two boundaries. Chrome supports since 2023.

qa.question: How does CSS Grid auto-placement algorithm work?
qa.answerHtml: When items don't have explicit grid-column/row, the auto-placement algorithm places them. grid-auto-flow: row (default): fills row by row. column: fills column by column. dense: backfills gaps with smaller items. grid-auto-rows/grid-auto-columns set implicit track sizes. Understanding auto-placement is key for masonry-like layouts.

qa.question: What are anchor-positioned elements?
qa.answerHtml: CSS Anchor Positioning (new spec): position elements relative to another element without JS. anchor-name: --my-anchor on target, position-anchor: --my-anchor on positioned element. top: anchor(bottom) positions below the anchor. Enables: tooltips, popovers, floating menus that follow their trigger. Replaces Floating UI/Popper.js for many cases.

qa.question: What is the inherit, initial, unset, revert values?
qa.answerHtml: inherit: use parent's value. initial: use CSS specification default. unset: inherit if property normally inherits, else initial. revert: use browser's default stylesheet value (not CSS spec). revert-layer: revert to previous @layer. Use revert to reset to browser defaults, unset for a cleaner approach.

qa.question: What is the min-height vs height difference?
qa.answerHtml: height: 500px is FIXED — content overflows if it's taller. min-height: 500px sets a minimum but GROWS with content. Use min-height for most layouts (especially min-height: 100dvh for full-page sections). Use fixed height only for explicitly sized elements (images, avatars).

qa.question: What is the line-height property?
qa.answerHtml: Sets the height of a line box. Unitless value recommended: line-height: 1.6 (1.6× the font size). With units: line-height: 24px (fixed, doesn't scale). Unitless values are inherited as a multiplier; unit values are inherited as the computed value. This is why line-height: 1.6 is better than line-height: 24px.

qa.question: What is the outline vs border difference?
qa.answerHtml: border: part of the box model (affects layout, adds to element size with content-box). outline: drawn OUTSIDE the border, does NOT affect layout, can't have radius (until recently), can be offset with outline-offset. Focus outlines: never remove them (outline: none) without a visible replacement. outline-offset: 2px looks better than touching the element.

qa.question: What is the pointer-events property?
qa.answerHtml: pointer-events: none makes element invisible to mouse/touch (clicks pass through). pointer-events: auto restores. Use for: disabled overlays, decorative elements that shouldn't block clicks, custom cursors/tooltips that shouldn't interfere. Works on SVG too with additional values (fill, stroke, etc.).

qa.question: What is the isolation property?
qa.answerHtml: isolation: isolate creates a new stacking context without side effects (unlike opacity, transform, or z-index which also change rendering). Use when you want to contain z-index without affecting the element's appearance. Common: isolate a component so its internal z-indexes don't leak to siblings.

qa.question: What is the place-items / place-content / place-self shorthand?
qa.answerHtml: place-items: center = align-items: center; justify-items: center. place-content: center = align-content: center; justify-content: center. place-self: center = align-self: center; justify-self: center. display: grid; place-items: center; is the shortest centering in CSS — 2 properties to center anything.

qa.question: What is the writing-mode property?
qa.answerHtml: writing-mode: vertical-rl makes text flow top-to-bottom, right-to-left (Chinese, Japanese). vertical-lr: left-to-right columns. horizontal-tb: normal (default). Changes how block/inline axes work. Logical properties (margin-block, padding-inline) automatically adapt. Used for: vertical text, sideways headings, CJK layouts.

qa.question: Explain the paint, layout, and composite rendering stages.
qa.answerHtml: Layout (Reflow): Calculates geometry of ALL affected elements — triggered by: width/height/margin/padding changes, DOM modifications, font loading. EXPENSIVE. Paint: Fills pixels — triggered by: color, background, shadow, visibility changes. Moderate cost. Composite: Combines layers on GPU — triggered by: transform, opacity ONLY. CHEAP. Goal: animate ONLY transform/opacity to stay in composite-only. Use will-change to promote elements to their own layer.

qa.question: What is CSS Containment?
qa.answerHtml: contain: layout: element's internal layout doesn't affect external. contain: paint: content doesn't render outside bounds. contain: size: element's size doesn't depend on children. contain: style: counters/quotes scoped. contain: content = layout + paint + style. contain: strict = all. Foundation of content-visibility. Tells browser it can skip work when recalculating — significant perf win for complex pages.

qa.question: What is CSS Grid masonry layout?
qa.answerHtml: Experimental: grid-template-rows: masonry creates Pinterest-style stacking where items fill vertical gaps. Currently only in Firefox (behind flag). Polyfills exist. Alternative: multi-column layout (column-count) — simpler but items flow top-to-bottom per column, not left-to-right. JavaScript libraries (Masonry.js) are still the reliable cross-browser solution in 2026.

qa.question: How does the new CSS @starting-style rule work?
qa.answerHtml: @starting-style defines initial styles for elements entering the DOM, enabling transitions for elements that start with display: none. Previously impossible — transitions need a starting state. @starting-style { dialog[open] { opacity: 0; } } dialog[open] { opacity: 1; transition: opacity 0.3s; }. Enables CSS-only modal/tooltip entry animations without JavaScript.

qa.question: What is the interpolate-size property?
qa.answerHtml: interpolate-size: allow-keywords on :root enables transitioning between keyword sizes (height: 0 → height: auto) — previously impossible without JavaScript. This means CSS-only accordions with smooth height animations: .content { height: 0; overflow: hidden; transition: height 0.3s; } .open .content { height: auto; }. Game-changing for collapsible content.
